creators.name: Nick Saraev
creators.blurb: Practical AI automation and agency builds from someone shipping real systems.

videos.title: I Built a $1M/y SaaS with Claude Code, Here's How
videos.summary: Nick Saraev describes building Clairvo, a $1M/year SaaS that uses Claude Code-generated techniques like predictive pacing and local number rotation to improve call pickup rates. He argues that using Claude to enumerate opportunities and practical implementation of AI agents is viable for building real-world revenue-generating businesses, contrasting his approach with many who talk automation without results.

videos.title: How to Build Mobile Apps with Claude Code: Full Course (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev's full-length course teaches mobile app development using Claude Code and Expo/React Native. The course covers setup, app design frameworks, building three complete apps (habit tracker, calorie tracker with AI, Pomodoro timer), database integration with Supabase, user authentication, and app store submission.

videos.title: How to Use Claude Code for FREE (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev demonstrates how to use Claude Code for free or at minimal cost by routing through NVIDIA-NIM, OpenRouter, Ollama for local models, and alternatives like DeepSeek V4 Flash. The video includes setup instructions for each method and references a full 4-hour Claude Code course.

videos.title: Claude Opus-4.7 Just Dropped, And...
videos.summary: Claude Opus-4.7 is the latest version of Anthropic's Claude model. Nick Saraev discusses the release and its applications in a YouTube video covering practical automation workflows and business use cases.

creators.name: AI Engineer
creators.blurb: Talks and sessions from the AI Engineer conference and community.

videos.title: Why Can't Anyone Answer Questions About the Business? — Garrett Galow, WorkOS
videos.summary: Garrett Galow from WorkOS presented Studio, an internal workspace that lets non-technical users ask business questions in natural language against Snowflake, Linear, and Notion, generating reusable widgets as declarative JavaScript. The system uses preflight sequencing for schema injection, explicit distrust of model knowledge, and query validation to ensure reliability for production use.

videos.title: The agent-ready web: Simplify user actions with WebMCP — Tara Agyemang, Google
videos.summary: WebMCP is a proposed web standard introduced by Google's Chrome team that enables AI agents to interact with websites through structured, named tools instead of parsing DOM and pixel coordinates. It provides both declarative HTML attributes and imperative JavaScript APIs for registering agent-callable actions, demonstrated with a concert ticket purchase flow.

videos.title: How to Keep Shipping When You Walk Away from Your Desk — Zack Proser, WorkOS
videos.summary: Zack Proser argues that human attention, not agent speed, is the bottleneck in AI-assisted development. He presents a four-layer stack of signal agents, verification gates, self-improvement loops, and holistic monitoring to enable developers to ship code while away from their desks, framing this as a solution to AI-induced burnout.

videos.title: Stop Making Models Bigger, Make Them Behave — Kobie Crawdord, Snorkel
videos.summary: A 4B model finetuned with RL using tool discipline (calling get_table_name, inspecting schema, self-correcting) outperformed Qwen 3 235B on SQL query tasks. The approach emphasizes structured tool use over model scale, with single-table training transferring to multi-table problems (13.9% to 26.6% on FinQA) at under $500 training cost.

creators.name: Cole Medin
creators.blurb: Practical AI agent and coding-assistant builds, end to end.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 is Now BANNED?!
videos.summary: Cole Medin discusses the reported shutdown of Claude Fable 5 by US government order three days after launch, attributed to export-control directives and claimed jailbreak vulnerability. The video covers why the model was pulled, implications for developers, and benchmark comparisons against Opus 4.8.

videos.title: Google's Agents CLI: The CLI + Skills Combination to Ship AI Agents EASILY
videos.summary: Google's Agents CLI is an open-source command-line tool that simplifies building and deploying production-grade AI agents. It provides agents with identity, sandboxed code execution, audit trails, and integrates with AI coding assistants like Claude Code.

videos.title: Live AI Q&A + Crushing it in Chess at the Same Time - Come Hang Out!
videos.summary: Cole Medin is hosting a live AI Q&A session on Saturdays where viewers can ask questions about AI coding, agents, Claude Code, and Archon. The session combines discussion with chess gameplay and is part of ongoing content for the Dynamous community.

videos.title: Claude Plans, Gemini Designs: The Workflow to Build BEAUTIFUL Frontends
videos.summary: Cole Medin demonstrates a workflow that routes frontend development tasks to specialized models: Claude Opus for planning and reasoning, Gemini 3.5 Flash for UI design and code generation. The approach leverages each model's strengths to build full-stack applications more efficiently and cost-effectively than using a single model.

creators.name: AI Jason
creators.blurb: AI app and agent builds, experiments, and walkthroughs.

videos.title: How to build proactive agents & self-improving company (Fully explained)
videos.summary: Video tutorial on constructing proactive AI agents and self-improving systems. Covers the fundamentals of proactive AI loops, demonstrates use cases in autonomous ad optimization and SEO, and discusses enabling technologies including Loopany framework, memory systems, and tool integration.

videos.title: Ralph-loop 2.0? The real autonomous coder is coming...
videos.summary: AI Jason discusses the /goal feature and /mission exploration capabilities for autonomous coding workflows. The video covers setup, prompting practices, and long-horizon task execution, positioning these as steps toward autonomous coder systems.

videos.title: New AI coding paradiagm - OpenAI Symphony
videos.summary: AI Jason covers OpenAI Symphony, a new coding paradigm that includes harness setup and auto testing capabilities. The video demonstrates Symphony integration with workflow configuration and mentions related tools like Crewlet and Superdesign.

videos.title: Okay, this unleashed my agent
videos.summary: AI Jason reviews several AI agent systems including Autoresearch, AutoAgent, Claude's 3-layer memory architecture, Hermes self-evolve mechanism, and Openclaw self-evolve skills. The video covers how these tools enable agent capabilities through memory management and skill evolution.

creators.name: Caleb Writes Code
creators.blurb: Hands-on coding sessions and software build-alongs.

videos.title: NVIDIA’s Nemotron 3 Is... Awesome?
videos.summary: A technical deep dive into NVIDIA's Nemotron 3 model series, examining the architectural innovations that differentiate them from other open models. The video analyzes three key components: Hybrid Mamba, Latent Mixture of Experts, and MTP.

videos.title: MiniMax M3 explained in 8min..
videos.summary: MiniMax released M3, a language model featuring MiniMax Sparse Attention (MSA) that replaces full attention with sparse attention. The model incorporates tiled improvements and optimized KV cache reading to reduce latency in prefill and decode stages during inference.

videos.title: Pi Agent explained in 6min..
videos.summary: Pi is an agent framework that enables unique approaches to building AI systems. The video compares Pi's architecture and capabilities against frontier alternatives like Claude Code, Cursor, and others in the agentic coding space.

videos.title: Huawei makes 1.4nm chip??
videos.summary: Caleb Writes Code discusses Huawei's announcement of Tau Scaling Law as a competitive response in the US-China semiconductor race. The video covers EUV and DUV lithography techniques, packaging innovations, and logic folding as key enablers of advanced chip manufacturing at the 1.4nm scale.

creators.name: AWS Developers
creators.blurb: Official AWS tutorials, service deep dives, and developer demos.

videos.title: AI and the Job Market in 2026
videos.summary: AWS Developers discusses job hunting strategies in 2026 amid AI screening of resumes. Covers networking value, personal branding via blogging and open source contribution, and argues that AI will evolve rather than eliminate junior developer roles. Recommends building projects and maintaining curiosity as core career strategies.

videos.title: 5 Tips for Deploying AI Agents to Production
videos.summary: AWS Developers outlines five production deployment patterns for AI agents: streaming tool call events for UI responsiveness, layered authentication (OAuth plus IAM), type-safe contracted database access with tenant isolation, guardrails with cost budgets and model routing, and agent observability via metrics and distributed tracing. These patterns apply across frameworks and platforms.

videos.title: Infrastructure as Code for Beginners (AWS CDK)
videos.summary: A tutorial on Infrastructure as Code fundamentals using AWS CDK in Python. Demonstrates building a serverless REST API by first manually clicking through the AWS console, then rebuilding it declaratively with CDK, covering DynamoDB, Lambda, and API Gateway. Contrasts CDK with alternative IaC tools like CloudFormation and Terraform.

videos.title: Stop AI Hallucinations With These 5 Techniques
videos.summary: AWS Developers outlines five techniques to reduce hallucinations in AI agents: semantic tool selection to filter context, graph RAG for precise queries, multi-agent validation to catch failures, symbolic guardrails via Python code, and runtime steering for self-correction. Demonstrations use an open-source travel agent and highlight Amazon Bedrock AgentCore's production implementation.

creators.name: Visual AI
creators.blurb: Visual walkthroughs and demos across modern AI tools.

creators.name: Alberta Tech
creators.blurb: Developer tutorials and practical builds in AI and software.

videos.title: Do Google engineers actually vibe code?
videos.summary: A video discussing whether Google engineers use code generation at scale, with comparisons between OpenAI Codex and Claude Code capabilities. The content examines the adoption and viability of automated code generation practices at Google across 2024 to 2026.

videos.title: This company killed open source
videos.summary: Alberta Tech discusses Malus, a company or tool that the creator argues is ending open source development. The video covers how Malus works and explores implications for open source maintainers going forward.

videos.title: I let Claude Code take my job (it did better than me)
videos.summary: Alberta Tech presents an experiment testing whether Claude Code can perform a software engineering task better than the creator. The video documents workflow, technical execution, and comparative results, raising questions about AI displacement in professional work.

creators.name: EO
creators.blurb: Conversations and stories with founders and builders in tech.

videos.title: How to Steal Customers From Giants 600x Bigger than Your Startup | Pigment, Eléonore Crespo
videos.summary: Eléonore Crespo, co-founder of Pigment, discusses how her AI performance management platform competes against legacy software giants by consolidating finance, supply chain, HR, and sales data. She argues that even innovative companies like Google rely on inadequate tools like spreadsheets for strategic planning, and that building a complete enterprise platform from day one while leveraging customer trust from respected companies is more effective than incremental feature releases.

videos.title: The Fastest Way to Know if Your Product Market Fit Is Real | Serval CEO, Jake Stauch
videos.summary: Jake Stauch, CEO of Serval, argues that passionate customers do not prove product-market fit and that real PMF manifests as customer pull rather than founder push. He advocates for embedded customer relationships over formal interviews and warns founders to distinguish informed AI bets from convenient delusions.

videos.title: He Raised $26M Betting on Probabilities, Not Guesses | Koah Labs, Nic Baird
videos.summary: Nic Baird, founder of Koah Labs, argues that ad-based monetization is a superior model for AI tools over subscription-only approaches that dominated VC thinking in 2024. He raises that the real bottleneck for AI agents is human trust adoption, not technology itself.

videos.title: I Hit $1M ARR in 117 Days. Bootstrapped to $10M. Here's My Playbook | Chatbase, Yasser Elsaid
videos.summary: Yasser Elsaid, founder of Chatbase, shares his bootstrapping strategy for reaching $10M ARR without external funding. The video covers lessons from hitting $1M ARR in 117 days, including product-led growth, SEO strategy, churn reduction, pricing experimentation, and decision-making frameworks for scaling from zero to ten million.

creators.name: Cyril Imhof
creators.blurb: AI automation and workflow builds for real projects.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 Now Banned in Europe by US Government (EU-Breakdown)
videos.summary: A video by Cyril Imhof discusses the claimed ban of Claude Fable 5 in Europe by the US government. The video references regulatory developments concerning Anthropic's model access across EU jurisdictions.

videos.title: The Ultimate Second Brain for AI Entrepreneurs: Fable 5 Claude with Loops
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof discusses Fable 5, an Anthropic model designed for AI entrepreneurs, highlighting its features, guardrails, memory capabilities, and agentic payment integration compared to Mythos 5.

videos.title: Anthropic's Brand New AI Just Started the Gold Rush (Fable 5)
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof discusses Anthropic's new Claude Fable 5 model and its applications in regulated industries. The video covers new use cases for the model and introduces "Know Your Agent" methodology for effective context engineering in long-running AI applications.

creators.name: PY
creators.blurb: Software engineering, tooling, and developer-workflow deep dives.
